title: SQL Lab 5: First and Last Order per Customer
short_description: Collapse order history to first and last order dates per customer.
business_context: Lifecycle reports frequently need customer acquisition and most recent activity dates.
problem_statement: For each customer, return their first_order_date and last_order_date.
student_task: Write a query returning customer_id, first_order_date, and last_order_date.
learning_goals: Use MIN and MAX for date boundaries
Avoid unnecessary window logic when aggregation is enough
broken_code:
SELECT customer_id, order_date AS first_order_date, order_date AS last_order_date
FROM orders;
production_logs: lifecycle-report: multiple rows emitted per customer
review-note: output grain should be one row per customer
solution_query:
SELECT customer_id,
       MIN(order_date) AS first_order_date,
       MAX(order_date) AS last_order_date
FROM orders
GROUP BY customer_id;
explanation: The requested grain is one row per customer, so grouped MIN and MAX directly answer the question.
common_mistakes: Using window functions but forgetting DISTINCT
hints: GROUP BY customer_id with MIN and MAX.
